etymology.description: Depicts the head of an animal with a long mouth and horns, often shown with or without hair ( 巛) -> perpetuated as 𩠐 (form with hair) -> streamlined to 首
etymology.interpretation: The character depicts the head of an animal, with prominent features like a long mouth and horns, emphasizing the notion of a 'head' both physically and symbolically as the foremost part.

examples.chinese: 他是这个团队的首领(他是這個團隊的首領)。
examples.pinyin: Tā shì zhège tuánduì de shǒulǐng.
examples.english: He is the head of this team.

examples.chinese: 这首歌(這首歌)是我最喜欢的歌曲(是我最喜歡的歌曲)。
examples.pinyin: Zhè shǒu gē shì wǒ zuì xǐhuān de gēqǔ.
examples.english: This song is my favorite track.

examples.chinese: 他抬起头(他抬起頭)，看向远方的山(看向遠方的山)。
examples.pinyin: Tā tái qǐ tóu, kàn xiàng yuǎnfāng de shān.
examples.english: He raised his head and looked towards the distant mountains.
